title: Elastic-net regularised regression with cross-validated λ selection
summary: Pure-Rust port of R's glmnet 4.1: elastic-net coordinate descent across a log-spaced λ sequence with k-fold cross-validation. Reports lambda.min and lambda.1se for every run — the downstream panel picks whichever it prefers. Supports all six glmnet families: gaussian, binomial, poisson, multinomial, mgaussian, cox.
description: Standardises predictors (and response for Gaussian/mgaussian) using glmnet's biased 1/n SD formula, builds λ_max = max_j|xⱼᵀy|/(n·max(α,1e-3)), log-spaces down by lambda_min_ratio, and fits every λ with warm starts and an active-set strategy (cycle active coordinates until stable, full KKT sweep, repeat). Gaussian runs direct weighted-least-squares CD; binomial/poisson/multinomial run IRLS (partial-Newton majorization) with CD as the inner solve; mgaussian uses block-CD with a group-lasso penalty across response columns; Cox uses Newton on the Breslow partial-likelihood with CD for each step. k-fold CV refits each fold at the shared master λ grid, scores held-out observations with the requested measure, and aggregates by weighted mean/SE across folds. Outputs: CV curve CSV (λ, cvm, cvsd, cvup, cvlo, nzero, is_min, is_1se), one coefficient-matrix CSV per response class (rows = predictors with an intercept row; columns = λ), and an XLSX workbook with summary + CV sheet + coef sheets.
usage:
[["model-glmnet::fit"]]
input = "./data.csv"
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "g1"
family = "gaussian"
y = "y"
alpha = 0.5
nlambda = 100
nfolds = 10

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: Numeric CSV of predictors plus response column(s). One row per observation.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Base directory. Outputs land in `<run_dir>/ocinfo/<run_name>_model_glmnet/`.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run; no '..', '/' or '\\'.

arguments.name: family
arguments.description: One of gaussian|binomial|poisson|multinomial|mgaussian|cox.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: Response column name, or comma list for mgaussian. Required unless family=cox.

arguments.name: time
arguments.description: Cox only: event/observation time column.

arguments.name: status
arguments.description: Cox only: 0/1 event-status column.

arguments.name: vars
arguments.description: Predictor column subset. Empty = every non-response/non-survival column.

arguments.name: alpha
arguments.description: Elastic-net mixing (1 = lasso, 0 = ridge).

arguments.name: nlambda
arguments.description: Number of λ values on the path.

arguments.name: lambda_min_ratio
arguments.description: λ_min/λ_max. Default 0.01 when n≥p else 1e-4.

arguments.name: lambda
arguments.description: Optional user-specified λ sequence (descending).

arguments.name: nfolds
arguments.description: Number of CV folds.

arguments.name: foldid
arguments.description: Optional CSV containing a single integer column of fold assignments (1..nfolds).

arguments.name: type_measure
arguments.description: CV loss: default|mse|mae|deviance|class|auc|c. Compatibility: gaussian↦mse/mae/deviance; binomial↦deviance/mse/mae/class/auc; poisson↦deviance/mse/mae; multinomial↦deviance/class/mse/mae; mgaussian↦mse/mae/deviance; cox↦deviance/c.

arguments.name: standardize
arguments.description: Standardise predictors to unit biased SD before fitting.

arguments.name: intercept
arguments.description: Include an intercept (Cox ignores this).

arguments.name: thresh
arguments.description: Coordinate-descent convergence threshold.

arguments.name: maxit
arguments.description: Maximum CD iterations per λ.

arguments.name: seed
arguments.description: RNG seed for fold assignment. Ignored if `foldid` is provided.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether CSV inputs have a header row.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Number of worker threads for per-fold CV. `1` (default) runs folds serially. Each fold fits an independent coordinate-descent path on a disjoint training subset, so parallel fan-out scales near-linearly up to `nfolds`.

arguments.name: output_format
arguments.description: Override stdout format: human, protocol, or quiet.

notes: Outputs always report both lambda.min (best CV score) and lambda.1se (largest λ whose cvm is within 1 SE of the best). Downstream consumers pick whichever they want.
Multinomial uses the `ungrouped` symmetric softmax parametrisation (matches glmnet `type.multinomial="ungrouped"`, the default).
Cox has no intercept — it is absorbed into the non-parametric baseline hazard and not identifiable.
Fold assignment is deterministic: either user-supplied via `foldid` CSV or synthesised from `seed` using a seeded Fisher–Yates shuffle that is stable across platforms.
